attrs.alt: Entra ID Workbooks and Insights architecture

content.text: Azure Monitor Workbooks are interactive, parameterised dashboards built on KQL queries. Microsoft Entra ID ships a gallery of built-in workbooks accessible in the Entra portal under Monitoring → Workbooks. They visualise sign-in patterns, Conditional Access coverage, MFA registration progress, authentication method adoption, and identity risk trends — all sourced from the Log Analytics workspace linked via diagnostic settings.

Built-in workbooks require a Log Analytics workspace linked to your Entra tenant with SignInLogs, AuditLogs, and RiskyUsers diagnostic categories enabled. Without this link, the gallery opens but all queries return empty results.
The following workbooks are available once a Log Analytics workspace is linked. Each targets a distinct operational question.
Failed sign-ins by app, user, location; error code distribution
Which CA policies applied per sign-in; grant/block breakdown
Which sign-ins had no CA policy applied (coverage gaps)
% users registered for MFA; SSPR registration vs usage
Which auth methods are used; legacy auth volume
Risk level distribution; risk state transitions over time
App proxy connector health; backend server errors
Confirm diagnostic settings are active and the SignInLogs table is populated before opening workbooks for the first time.

# Verify diagnostic settings are forwarding Entra logs to Log Analytics
Invoke-RestMethod `
  -Uri 'https://management.azure.com/providers/microsoft.aadiam/diagnosticSettings?api-version=2017-04-01-preview' `
  -Headers @{Authorization="Bearer $token"} | Select-Object -ExpandProperty value |
  ForEach-Object { Write-Host $_.name, $_.properties.workspaceId }

# Confirm SignInLogs table exists in Log Analytics (should return rows after ~15 min)
$query = 'SignInLogs | take 1'
Invoke-AzOperationalInsightsQuery -WorkspaceId '<law-id>' -Query $query |
  Select-Object -ExpandProperty Results

The Gap Analyzer workbook identifies sign-ins where no Conditional Access policy evaluated — the most critical coverage gap in an identity security posture. The underlying KQL query can be used in Log Analytics alert rules to trigger notifications when uncovered sign-ins exceed a threshold.

// Find sign-ins where no CA policy applied (coverage gap)
SignInLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ResultType == 0  // successful sign-ins only
| where ConditionalAccessStatus == 'notApplied'
| where UserType == 'Member'
| summarize Count = count(), Apps = make_set(AppDisplayName, 20) by UserPrincipalName
| order by Count desc
| take 50

The UserRegistrationDetails table (P2 tenants) provides per-user MFA and SSPR registration state without relying on sign-in sampling. Use it to find administrators who have not registered a strong authentication method — a compliance gap in most security frameworks.

// Admins who have NOT registered a strong authentication method (P2 required)
UserRegistrationDetails
| where IsAdmin == true or IsPrivilegedRole == true
| where IsMfaRegistered == false
| project UserPrincipalName, UserDisplayName, IsAdmin, IsPrivilegedRole
| order by IsAdmin desc

Legacy authentication protocols bypass Conditional Access entirely. The Authentication Methods workbook surfaces legacy volume; the KQL below provides the per-protocol breakdown needed to scope and justify a block-legacy-auth CA policy.

// Legacy authentication protocol breakdown — input for block-legacy-auth CA policy
SignInLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where ClientAppUsed in ('Exchange ActiveSync','IMAP4','POP3','SMTP Auth',
                          'Other clients','Authenticated SMTP','AutoDiscover',
                          'Exchange Web Services','MAPI Over HTTP','Outlook Anywhere')
| summarize Count = count(), Users = dcount(UserPrincipalName)
    by ClientAppUsed, AppDisplayName
| order by Count desc
Track the shift from passwords toward passwordless authentication over time. This query produces the daily trend data underlying the Authentication Methods Activity workbook chart.

// Authentication method distribution for successful sign-ins
SignInLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where ResultType == 0
| extend MfaMethod = tostring(MfaDetail.authMethod)
| summarize Count = count() by MfaMethod
| order by Count desc

// Daily trend: passwordless vs password sign-ins
SignInLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where ResultType == 0
| extend AuthType = case(
    AuthenticationDetails[0].authenticationMethod == 'FIDO2 security key', 'Passwordless-FIDO2',
    AuthenticationDetails[0].authenticationMethod == 'Microsoft Authenticator', 'Passwordless-Auth',
    AuthenticationDetails[0].authenticationMethod == 'Password', 'Password',
    'Other')
| summarize Count = count() by AuthType, bin(TimeGenerated, 1d)
| order by TimeGenerated asc

The Risky Sign-Ins workbook visualises risk level distribution and state transitions. These two queries reproduce the most actionable panels: overall risk distribution and high-risk sign-ins that still succeeded without remediation.

// Sign-in risk level distribution
SignInLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where RiskLevelDuringSignIn != 'none'
| summarize Count = count() by RiskLevelDuringSignIn, RiskState
| order by Count desc

// High/medium risk sign-ins that succeeded — investigate these
SignInLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ResultType == 0
| where RiskLevelDuringSignIn in ('medium','high')
| project TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, RiskLevelDuringSignIn,
          AppDisplayName, IPAddress, Location
| order by TimeGenerated desc

Custom workbooks are JSON documents stored as Azure resources. Each item in the items array is a workbook element: type 9 is a parameter row, type 3 is a query/chart, type 1 is a Markdown text block. Parameters are referenced in KQL using curly brace syntax: {ParameterName}.
Deploy workbooks as code so they survive tenant migrations and can be reproduced across environments. Store the serializedData JSON in source control alongside CA policies and diagnostic settings.

# Deploy a custom workbook via Azure CLI (workbook JSON stored in file)
az resource create
  --resource-group rg-identity
  --resource-type microsoft.insights/workbooks
  --api-version 2022-04-01
  --name $(python3 -c "import uuid; print(uuid.uuid4())")
  --properties '{
    "displayName": "Entra Identity Overview",
    "category": "workbook",
    "serializedData": "<workbook-json-string>",
    "sourceId": "/subscriptions/<sub>/resourceGroups/<rg>/providers/Microsoft.OperationalInsights/workspaces/<law>"
  }'
Export an existing workbook's definition to JSON for Git-based version control. Use this after editing workbooks in the portal to capture the latest state.

# Export a workbook definition to JSON for version control
$workbookResourceId = '/subscriptions/<sub>/resourceGroups/<rg>/providers/microsoft.insights/workbooks/<id>'

az rest --method GET `
  --url "https://management.azure.com${workbookResourceId}?api-version=2022-04-01" |
  ConvertFrom-Json |
  Select-Object -ExpandProperty properties |
  ConvertTo-Json -Depth 20 | Out-File workbook-export.json

Write-Host 'Workbook exported to workbook-export.json'
Workbooks vs Azure Dashboards vs Sentinel Workbooks
The SSPR Usage and Insights workbook shows reset success rates by authentication method. The KQL below reproduces the core metric for use in custom dashboards or alert rules.

// SSPR success vs failure by reset method over last 30 days
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where LoggedByService == 'Self-service Password Management'
| extend Method = tostring(AdditionalDetails[?(@.key == 'ResetMethod')].value)
| extend Actor  = tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName)
| summarize
    Success = countif(Result == 'success'),
    Failure = countif(Result == 'failure')
    by Method
| extend SuccessRate = round(100.0 * Success / (Success + Failure), 1)
| order by Success desc

The Application Proxy Activity workbook surfaces backend server errors for on-premises apps published through Entra App Proxy. Use this query to find apps with high error rates before users report them.

// Application Proxy backend server errors — identify unhealthy on-premises apps
SignInLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where AppDisplayName has 'Application Proxy'
| where ResultType != 0
| extend BackendError = tostring(Status.additionalDetails)
| summarize Count = count(), Users = dcount(UserPrincipalName)
    by AppDisplayName, ResultDescription, BackendError
| order by Count desc

In multi-tenant or MSSP environments, a single workbook can query multiple Log Analytics workspaces using the union workspace() syntax. This enables a single consolidated identity risk dashboard across tenants without duplicating workbooks.

// Cross-workspace query: correlate sign-in risk events across multiple tenants
// (useful in MSSP or multi-tenant Sentinel scenarios)
union workspace('law-tenant-a').SignInLogs,
      workspace('law-tenant-b').SignInLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1d)
| where RiskLevelDuringSignIn in ('medium','high')
| extend Tenant = tostring(TenantId)
| summarize Count = count() by Tenant, RiskLevelDuringSignIn
| order by Count desc

Cross-workspace queries require the querying identity to have the Log Analytics Reader role on every referenced workspace. Use managed identity for Logic Apps or automation accounts to avoid credential management.

Workbooks are interactive and cannot natively send email reports. Replicate any workbook KQL query in a Logic App with a recurrence trigger and Log Analytics data action to deliver scheduled identity security digests to a distribution list.
Standard {TimeRange} filter applied to all KQL queries in the workbook
Select a specific user, app, or risk level to filter all tiles
Dynamically populate options from a KQL query (e.g. list of CA policies)
Free-text search filter (e.g. user UPN prefix)
Select Azure subscription for resource-scoped queries

Built-in Entra workbooks provide zero-configuration dashboards for sign-in analysis, CA coverage, MFA registration, authentication methods, and identity risk — once a Log Analytics workspace is linked via diagnostic settings.
The CA Gap Analyzer workbook is the fastest way to discover users and applications with no Conditional Access policy coverage.
The MFA Registration and Usage workbook tracks organisational MFA adoption and surfaces administrators who have not registered a strong method.
Legacy authentication volume queries provide the evidence needed to justify and scope a block-legacy-auth Conditional Access policy.
Custom workbooks are parameterised KQL dashboards stored as ARM resources — version-control the JSON alongside CA policies and diagnostic settings using the export procedure.
Workbooks are read-only visualisations; pair them with Log Analytics alert rules or Sentinel analytics rules to trigger automated responses on the same KQL logic.
